From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1978 comedy crime film

| Little Italy | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Directed by | [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") |
| Written by | [Mario Amendola](/wiki/Mario_Amendola "Mario Amendola")   Bruno Corbucci |
| Produced by | Galliano Juso |
| Starring | [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") |
| Cinematography | Marcello Masciocchi |
| Edited by | Daniele Alabiso |
| Music by | [Goblin](/wiki/Goblin_(band) "Goblin (band)") |
| Release date | * 1978 (1978) |
| Running time | 91 minutes |
| Country | Italy |
| Language | [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") |

***Squadra antimafia***, internationally released as ***Little Italy***, is a 1978 Italian "[poliziottesco](/wiki/Poliziotteschi "Poliziotteschi")"-[comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci"). It is the fourth chapter in the Nico Giraldi film series starred by [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-ccc-2)

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Little_Italy_(1978_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Tomas Milian](/wiki/Tomas_Milian "Tomas Milian") as Nico Giraldi
* [Enzo Cannavale](/wiki/Enzo_Cannavale "Enzo Cannavale") as Salvatore Esposito
* [Eli Wallach](/wiki/Eli_Wallach "Eli Wallach") as Don Girolamo Giarra
* [Bombolo](/wiki/Bombolo "Bombolo") as Venticello
* [Margherita Fumero](/wiki/Margherita_Fumero "Margherita Fumero") as Maria Sole Giarra
* [Massimo Vanni](/wiki/Massimo_Vanni "Massimo Vanni") as Brigadiere Gargiulo
* John P. Dulaney as Ballarin
* Tomas Milian, Jr. as Francesco
